claim.title: GWTC-3: Compact Binary Coalescences Observed by LIGO and Virgo During the Second Part of the Third Observing Run

status_reason.note: UNVERIFIED (LLM draft): GWTC-3 contains observations that are new and unprecedented
UNVERIFIED (LLM draft): The sample size is not insufficient as it includes the first confident observations of neutron star-black hole binaries.
[machine] no direct observation recorded (E2); recorded evidence: 1 direct (distinct sources: 1, of which PRIMARY: 1), 0 indirect/analog, 0 theoretical
UNVERIFIED (LLM draft): The literature on compact binary coalescences continues to grow rapidly with new discoveries and analyses.

evidence.description: The third Gravitational-Wave Transient Catalog (GWTC-3) describes signals detected with Advanced LIGO and Advanced Virgo up to the end of their third observing run. It presents candidate gravitational waves from compact binary coalescences during the second half of the third observing run.

open_questions: Can gravitational-wave data alone distinguish between neutron stars and black holes in the binary components?
What is the significance of observing neutron star-black hole binaries for our understanding of stellar evolution?

sources.label: arXiv-2111-03606

axis_derivation: recorded evidence: 1 direct (distinct sources: 1, of which PRIMARY: 1), 0 indirect/analog, 0 theoretical
rule E2: direct observation exists but not from two distinct PRIMARY sources -> single direct line
